purpose: Provides a monitorable checklist for tracking labor market transitions from resilience to recession with calibrated traversal times
components: Stage 1: Declining hires, stable layoffs (current position)
Stage 2: Hires stagnation, temp help contraction broadens
Stage 3: Initial claims rising, quits rate declining
Stage 4: Layoffs rising, continuing claims accelerating
Stage 5: Unemployment rate triggering Sahm Rule
current_assessment: Currently at Stage 1 with declining hires and stable layoffs. Temp help has been negative for 36 months without broader deterioration — either the indicator is structurally broken or fiscal extension is historically unprecedented. Standard traversal: 12-24 months; fiscal-supported: 18-36 months.
limitations: Temp help anomaly (36 months negative) is unresolved — could indicate structural break in the indicator
Fiscal support may extend traversal time beyond historical calibration
Transition signals between stages may be blurred by data quality deterioration
